text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, there was a wise old owl named Oliver. Despite his grey feathers and tired eyes, Oliver loved exploring the forests. He’d sit on a favourite branch, listening to the sounds of nightly life.

One particular evening, he noticed something odd. Every bird seemed hungover. Owls especially. Oliver laughed.

"Why such boozers?"

Then he heard it. A soft crooning.

There! That must be Luna, the graceful nocturnal owl. She loved singing.

Oliver followed moonlit trails. At last there she was. Plucking strings with her beak. Quite unlike her usual quiet.

"You’re late, huh?" he whispered.

She looked up. "Lost my buzz."

"The stars never sleep!"

"Nope. Why?"

"I've got music."

She smiled.

With sticks she fashioned guitars. Together they would travel. Their tales woven into the woods.

One night they heard tales of rainbows.

The next, stars traveling in tiny carts.

After
